_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-16 Licensure Level Requirement for Attendant (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Licensure Level Requirement for Attendant in Charge
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-16: Licensure Level Requirement for Attendant — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the minimum licensure level required for the Attendant in Charge (AIC) of an EMS unit within an MCA's jurisdiction. Michigan law defines minimum AIC requirements; MCAs may adopt higher standards. The AIC is responsible for patient care decisions and crew supervision during an EMS response.

sections.title: Attendant in Charge (AIC) Responsibilities
points: The AIC is the licensed EMS provider responsible for patient care on the responding unit
AIC responsibilities include: patient assessment and treatment decisions, crew supervision, documentation accountability, and communication with medical control
The AIC must be the highest-licensed provider on the unit, or as otherwise defined by MCA protocol
The AIC is responsible for ensuring that all care provided on the unit is within their scope and the scope of their crew members

sections.title: Minimum AIC Licensure
points: Michigan minimum: the AIC must hold a current Michigan EMS provider license at the appropriate level for the unit type
BLS unit: AIC must be licensed at minimum EMT level
ALS unit: AIC must be licensed at minimum Paramedic level
LALS unit: AIC must be licensed at minimum Specialist (AEMT) level
MCAs may adopt higher AIC licensure requirements within their jurisdiction

sections.title: MCA Higher Standards
points: MCA OPTIONAL — MCAs may establish higher licensure requirements for AIC within their jurisdiction
MCAs may require: Paramedic as AIC on all ALS calls regardless of response type, specific credentialing for interfacility AIC roles, or continuous AIC training/recertification requirements
Higher MCA standards must be documented in MCA protocols and communicated to all licensed EMS agencies within the MCA's jurisdiction

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may adopt higher AIC licensure standards beyond the Michigan minimum
options: Michigan minimum standards adopted (EMT for BLS; Specialist for LALS; Paramedic for ALS)
MCA higher standard adopted — specify in MCA protocols

scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL for higher standards — Michigan minimum applies by default
AIC: highest-licensed provider on unit; responsible for patient care decisions
MCA may adopt higher standards — document and communicate to agencies
AIC responsible for ensuring all care is within scope of crew members
